creator: vermaden@interia.pl
text:
Hi.

I have two PNY MicroSDXC 64 GB cards. I have tested them with multiple tools and  theny seem to be good, I have also tried a MicroSDXC card from other manufactureer (do not remember which) with 64 GB size and the result was the same.

When I use exFAT or NTFS filesystem (or ZFS/UFS) there is no problem.

I have directory for documentation with about 7500 files (PDF mostly) segregated info about 200 dirs and subdirs. The file names are ASCII, only small and big letters, spaces, numbers, hypens (-) and nothing more, no hard spaces or other strange things like that.

Its total uncompressed size is about 50 GB.

When I cp(1) or rsync(1) that dir onto exFAT/NTFS/ZFS/UFS filesystem, then everything is ok, but When I do the same onto msdosfs filesystem, it breaks.

I have tested this on ThinkPads T520 and W530, both in SD slots using adapter or in USB 2.0/3.0 slots using other adapter.

Everytime I copy or rsync that dir onto even newly created and mounted msdosfs filesystem I end up with something like this:

# dmesg | grep mmcsd0
mmcsd0: 63GB <SDHC SD64G 3.0 SN xxxxxxxx MFG 05/2016 by 39 PH> at mmc0 50.0MHz/4bit/65535-block

# which newfs_msdos
/sbin/newfs_msdos

# newfs_msdos -L PNY /dev/mmcsd0
newfs_msdos: cannot get number of sectors per track: No such file or directory
newfs_msdos: cannot get number of heads: No such file or directory
newfs_msdos: trim 33 sectors to adjust to a multiple of 63
/dev/mmcsd0: 122161920 sectors in 1908780 FAT32 clusters (32768 bytes/cluster)
BytesPerSec=512 SecPerClust=64 ResSectors=32 FATs=2 Media=0xf0 SecPerTrack=63 Heads=255 HiddenSecs=0 HugeSectors=122191839 FATsecs=14913 RootCluster=2 FSInfo=1 Backup=2

# ls -l /dev/mmcsd0 
crw-r-----  1 root  operator  0x84 2017.08.05 10:36 /dev/mmcsd0

# file -s /dev/mmcsd0
/dev/mmcsd0: DOS/MBR boot sector, code offset 0x58+2, OEM-ID "BSD4.4  ", sectors/cluster 64, sectors/track 63, heads 255, sectors 122191839 (volumes > 32 MB) , FAT (32 bit), sectors/FAT 14913, serial number 0xa70d1203, label: "PNY        "

# mount_msdosfs -o noatime -o large -o longnames -D cp852 -L pl_PL.UTF-8 -m 644 -M 755 -u 10000 -g 10000 /dev/mmcsd0 /media/mmcsd0

# cp -r ~/books /media/mmcsd0/

# du -sm /media/mmcsd0/books
